laneLabelNote: E2B is the primary-correctness Gemma-4 lane: first validation target, smallest dense variant, the shape that Doe's simulator + WebGPU + CSL emulator receipts anchor against. 31B is the dense scale-up; 26B/A4B MoE is a later blocked efficiency lane.
purpose: Freeze the canonical bundle that a real-weight Gemma-4 E2B layer-block parity run must match. Doppler WebGPU reference, Doe CSL simfabric, and the CSL WebGPU emulator all ingest the same manifest, graph, input, and weights. Every hash below is pinned so the parity harness can reject a drifted fixture at validate-time rather than debug a digest mismatch at parity-time.

modelShape.note: headDim=512 is the manifest's production shape. Current layer-block kernel runs the smoke shape (numHeads=8, headDim=8, kvLen=4, size=1024). A real-weight receipt at manifest shape requires the head-dim rewrite; until that lands, real-weight runs must be labeled 'real_weight_smoke_shape' and cannot claim manifest-shape execution.

input.note: Canonical initial_rows for the layer-block chain at smoke size 1024. Seeded-RNG fixture; every lane reads THIS file, not re-generates from seed.

weightsDir.contract: Two files per layer in [0, numLayers): per_layer_inputs.perLayerModelProjection.layer{l}.f32 and layer.{l}.smoke_layer_block_wts.f32. At smoke shape each file is size * 4 = 4096 bytes. At manifest shape (headDim=512) per-file bytes scale with num_heads * head_dim * 4. Validator cross-checks file sizes against modelShape; see bench/tools/validate_weights_dir.py.
weightsDir.expectedWeightSetSha256Note: Pinned from bench/tools/extract_gemma4_e2b_weight_slices.py materializing the current smoke-contract slices from the BF16 SafeTensors source discoverable through Doppler's local int4ple origin metadata.

parityPolicy.note: Per docs/claim-discipline.md cross-runtime threshold. Real-weight smoke outputs can be large because the contract slices raw checkpoint tensors into the reduced layer-block kernel, so the parity harness records both atol and rtol using abs(a-b) <= atol + rtol * max(abs(a), abs(b)). Chain-final comparison compounds drift; per-layer-isolated evaluation is the authoritative parity granularity.

fixtureChain.label: L1_single_layer_real_weight
fixtureChain.expectedOutputSha256Note: Populated once a real-weight L1 run produces it; pins cross-lane expected output for regression.

claimScope.claimable: Same bundle-identity run across three lanes with real weights, bit-exact or within-tolerance output parity at the stated chain depth.
notClaimable: Gemma-4 manifest-shape execution (until headDim-512 kernel lands)
Full end-to-end Gemma-4 inference (layer-block only, no embed/unembed/sample)
Cerebras hardware performance (until hardware_success receipt lands)

stopShipIf: bundle.manifest.sha256 or bundle.graph.sha256 diverges from recorded value
input.sha256 on disk diverges from recorded value
weightsDir.expectedWeightSetSha256 is set but a candidate dir produces a different sha
any lane reports a manifest/graph sha different from this fixture's bundle
